threats.title: Permanent Plot Bricking via transferToUnoccupiedPlot State Desync
threats.hypothesis_description: The transferToUnoccupiedPlot function fails to update the plotId in the toilerState mapping. This causes subsequent unstake operations to clear the old, already-empty plot while leaving the new plot permanently marked as occupied, destroying the landlord's plot capacity.
threats.investigation_rationale: State desynchronization between plotOccupied and toilerState allows any renter to permanently brick a landlord's plots, requiring immediate mitigation.

proof_of_signal.pattern: plotOccupied is updated for oldPlotId and plotId, but toilerState[tokenId].plotId is never updated

preconditions: Landlord has an unoccupied plot available

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: transferToUnoccupiedPlot updates occupancy mappings but misses the core toiler state update

operational_questions: Can a malicious renter systematically brick all plots of competing landlords?
validation_requirements: Verify toilerState.plotId remains unchanged after transfer
Verify unstake clears the old plot and leaves the new plot permanently occupied

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Stake a Munchable on a landlord's plot
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Call transferToUnoccupiedPlot to move to a new plot
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Call unstakeMunchable, which reads the old plotId from toilerState
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: The new plot remains permanently occupied with no way to clear it

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Permanent loss of plot capacity for landlords, destroying the protocol's core economic utility
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Requires contract upgrade to fix state mappings

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Calls to transferToUnoccupiedPlot followed by unstakeMunchable
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Plots marked as occupied but no corresponding toilerState pointing to them

threats.title: Permanent NFT Lock via lastUpdated Timestamp Underflow
threats.hypothesis_description: When a plot becomes invalid, _farmPlots uses the landlord's lastUpdated timestamp. If the renter farmed more recently than the landlord updated their metadata, timestamp - _toiler.lastToilDate underflows, reverting the transaction and permanently locking the NFT since unstake requires farming.
threats.investigation_rationale: A natural sequence of events (landlord inactivity followed by plot reduction) will permanently lock user NFTs due to an arithmetic underflow in a mandatory withdrawal path.

proof_of_signal.pattern: timestamp = plotMetadata[landlord].lastUpdated; followed by schnibblesTotal = (timestamp - _toiler.lastToilDate) * BASE_SCHNIBBLE_RATE;

preconditions: Landlord's plotMetadata.lastUpdated is older than renter's toilerState.lastToilDate
Landlord reduces locked value, making the renter's plotId invalid

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Fallback timestamp assignment can be older than the last toil date, causing underflow

operational_questions: How frequently do landlords update metadata vs renters farming?
validation_requirements: Test underflow condition when lastUpdated < lastToilDate
Verify unstakeMunchable reverts due to forceFarmPlots modifier

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Landlord locks value and updates metadata (lastUpdated set)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Renter stakes and farms frequently (lastToilDate > lastUpdated)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Landlord unlocks value, reducing plot count below renter's plotId
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Renter attempts to unstake, underflows, and NFT is locked forever
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: None (can happen naturally or via malicious landlord)

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Permanent loss of user NFTs locked in the contract
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Requires contract upgrade to rescue NFTs

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Failed unstake transactions with arithmetic underflow errors
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Users complaining about locked NFTs after landlord unlocks

threats.title: Systemic Reward Destruction via Incorrect Bonus Math Formula
threats.hypothesis_description: The schnibbles calculation formula divides the entire reward by 100 instead of adding the percentage bonus. This results in all users receiving approximately 1% of their intended base rewards, destroying the protocol's economic model.
threats.investigation_rationale: A fundamental math error in the core reward calculation affects all users unconditionally, requiring immediate correction.

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Formula calculates S * (1 + B) / 100 instead of S * (100 + B) / 100

operational_questions: Are there any compensating factors in BASE_SCHNIBBLE_RATE to offset this?
validation_requirements: Calculate expected vs actual rewards for various bonus levels
Verify base rewards are decimated when bonus is 0

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: User stakes and waits for rewards to accrue
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Contract calculates rewards but divides the total by 100
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: User receives a fraction of intended rewards

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Complete failure of the protocol's reward economy
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Requires contract upgrade and retroactive airdrops to fix

investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Mismatch between expected APY and actual APY

threats.title: Critical Misconfiguration via Incorrect Storage Keys in _reconfigure
threats.hypothesis_description: The _reconfigure function loads critical numeric configuration values (like MIN_TAX_RATE and PRICE_PER_PLOT) using StorageKeys intended for contract addresses (e.g., StorageKey.LockManager). This will load incorrect values or zeros, potentially causing division by zero and bricking the contract.
threats.investigation_rationale: Using address keys for uint values is a severe initialization defect that will corrupt core protocol parameters upon deployment or upgrade.
deployment_assumptions: ConfigStorage returns the address cast to uint, or 0 if the key is strictly typed
preconditions: Contract is initialized or configUpdated is called

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Mismatch between variable purpose (tax rate) and storage key name (LockManager)

operational_questions: What values does ConfigStorage actually return for these keys?
validation_requirements: Check ConfigStorage implementation for type safety
Verify if PRICE_PER_PLOT evaluates to 0, causing division by zero in _getNumPlots

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: All calls to _getNumPlots revert with division by zero, bricking staking and unstaking

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Complete DoS of the contract if PRICE_PER_PLOT is 0, or broken tax bounds
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Requires config update or contract upgrade

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Reverts during stake/unstake due to division by zero

threats.title: Unbacked Yield Generation via Dirty Flag Boundary Off-By-One
threats.hypothesis_description: The dirty flag check uses a strict less-than operator (_getNumPlots(landlord) < _toiler.plotId) instead of less-than-or-equal. This allows a renter on the exact boundary plot to continue farming indefinitely even after the landlord reduces their locked value and invalidates the plot.
threats.investigation_rationale: An off-by-one error in plot validation allows users to generate unbacked yield, inflating the reward supply.
preconditions: Renter is staked on plotId N
Landlord reduces locked value such that they have exactly N plots (valid plots 0 to N-1)

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Boundary check fails to invalidate the Nth plot when total plots is N

operational_questions: How much unbacked yield can be generated this way?
validation_requirements: Verify dirty flag is not set and rewards continue to accrue

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Landlord has N+1 plots. Renter stakes on plotId N.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Landlord withdraws value, reducing total plots to N.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Plot N is now invalid, but _getNumPlots (N) < plotId (N) is false.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Renter continues to farm and earn rewards on an unbacked plot.
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: Collusion between landlord and renter can maximize this.

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Inflation of schnibbles supply without required locked value backing
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Easy to fix via upgrade, but inflated rewards remain

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Farming on plotIds equal to the landlord's current plot count
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Yield generated exceeding the theoretical max for locked value
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Landlords frequently locking and unlocking to maintain boundary states

threats.title: Staking Limit Bypass via Off-By-One Error
threats.hypothesis_description: The stakeMunchable function checks if the user's staked count is strictly greater than 10 before allowing a new stake. This allows a user to stake an 11th Munchable when their count is exactly 10, bypassing the intended maximum limit.
threats.investigation_rationale: A classic off-by-one error allows users to exceed protocol limits, potentially gaining an unfair advantage in reward generation.
preconditions: User attempts to stake an 11th Munchable

operational_questions: Does the 11th staked Munchable cause any out-of-gas issues in loops?
validation_requirements: Verify a user can successfully stake 11 Munchables

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: User calls stakeMunchable for the 11th NFT
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Check passes (10 > 10 is false), and the 11th NFT is staked

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Minor inflation of rewards for power users

threats.title: Infinite Schnibbles Minting via Negative Bonus Underflow
threats.hypothesis_description: If the configured REALM_BONUSES or RARITY_BONUSES result in a combined finalBonus <= -100, the reward calculation numerator becomes negative. Casting this negative int256 to uint256 causes a massive underflow, minting near-infinite schnibbles to the user and landlord.
threats.investigation_rationale: Missing bounds checking on signed integer arithmetic before casting to unsigned integers can lead to catastrophic token inflation if configuration allows negative bonuses.
deployment_assumptions: Configured bonuses can sum to <= -100 (e.g., cross-realm penalties)
threats.config_evidence: LandManager.sol:_farmPlots applies REALM_BONUSES and RARITY_BONUSES without bounds checking
preconditions: Configured REALM_BONUSES and RARITY_BONUSES allow a combined value <= -100
User stakes an NFT with attributes that trigger this negative bonus

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Unsafe cast from potentially negative int256 to uint256
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Requires specific bonus configuration by admin
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: While negative bonus config is active

operational_questions: What are the actual configured values for REALM_BONUSES and RARITY_BONUSES?
validation_requirements: Verify underflow behavior with negative finalBonus
Check if uint8 rarity bonuses > 127 are misinterpreted as negative int8

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Admin configures bonuses with negative penalties
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: User identifies an NFT/Landlord combination yielding finalBonus <= -100
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Underflow occurs, crediting the user with ~2^256 schnibbles

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Complete destruction of the schnibbles economy via hyperinflation
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Requires contract upgrade and manual state correction of balances

investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Users seeking out heavily penalized realm combinations

threats.title: Landlord Tax Rate Bait-and-Switch Exploitation
threats.hypothesis_description: The updateTaxRate function does not force existing stakers to farm before applying the new rate. A landlord can attract renters with a 0% tax rate, wait for them to stake, and then raise the rate to the maximum. Renters will be taxed at the maximum rate for all future periods until they notice and unstake.
threats.investigation_rationale: Lack of forced state updates during parameter changes allows landlords to exploit renters through deceptive configuration changes.

proof_of_signal.pattern: updateTaxRate updates plotMetadata[landlord].currentTaxRate without calling _farmPlots for existing stakers

preconditions: Renters are staked on the landlord's plots
Landlord has the ability to change the tax rate

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Tax rate changes apply to future periods without settling past accruals first

operational_questions: Is there any off-chain monitoring to alert renters of tax rate changes?
validation_requirements: Verify that a tax rate change affects the next farming period for existing stakers
Confirm renters have no on-chain protection against sudden rate hikes

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Landlord sets tax rate to 0% to attract renters
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Landlord calls updateTaxRate to set rate to MAX_TAX_RATE
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Renters' subsequent farm calls lock in the MAX_TAX_RATE for their next period

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Loss of trust in the protocol, unfair extraction of renter yield
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Cannot recover lost yield without landlord cooperation

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Frequent tax rate changes by landlords
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Landlords baiting with low rates and switching to high rates
